name: Zambia Sugar PLC
description: Zambia Sugar is the country's largest sugar producer and a subsidiary of Associated British Foods (ABF), which holds 75% of the equity. The company crossed the $1 billion market capitalization threshold in January 2026, joining the LuSE's exclusive 'Three Comma Club' alongside CEC and ZCCM-IH.

analystNotes.note: Crossed $1B valuation. But K89,926 daily trading value (~$2,000) on a billion-dollar company is the 'Paperweight Heavyweight' problem in one ticker. 75% ABF lock-up means the market cap is decorative.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

stance: AVOID FOR TRADING — Great company, terrible trading stock
